standard.name: SDTM
standard.description: Study Data Tabulation Model Implementation Guide v3.4

classes.name: Special Purpose
classes.description: Domains that do not conform to one of the three general observation classes

classes.name: Interventions
classes.description: Data that reflect study treatments, such as drug exposure or procedures

classes.name: Events
classes.description: Data that reflect occurrences, such as adverse events or disposition

classes.name: Findings
classes.description: Data that reflect observations, such as laboratory tests or vital signs

classes.name: Trial Design
classes.description: Domains describing the design of the clinical trial

domains.name: Demographics
domains.description: One record per subject. Contains demographic and baseline information.

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies for all applications

variables.name: SUBJID
variables.label: Subject Identifier for the Study
variables.description: Subject identifier within a study

variables.name: RFSTDTC
variables.label: Subject Reference Start Date/Time
variables.description: Reference start date/time for the subject in ISO 8601

variables.name: RFENDTC
variables.label: Subject Reference End Date/Time
variables.description: Reference end date/time for the subject in ISO 8601

variables.name: RFXSTDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of First Study Treatment
variables.description: First date/time of study treatment exposure

variables.name: RFXENDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Last Study Treatment
variables.description: Last date/time of study treatment exposure

variables.name: RFICDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Informed Consent
variables.description: Date/time of informed consent in ISO 8601

variables.name: RFPENDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of End of Participation
variables.description: Date/time of the end of a subject's participation in a study

variables.name: DTHDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Death
variables.description: Date/time of death of the subject in ISO 8601

variables.name: DTHFL
variables.label: Subject Death Flag
variables.description: Indicates the subject died (Y or N)

variables.name: SITEID
variables.label: Study Site Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a site

variables.name: BRTHDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Birth
variables.description: Date/time of birth

variables.name: AGE
variables.label: Age
variables.description: Age at reference start date

variables.name: AGEU
variables.label: Age Units
variables.description: Units associated with AGE

variables.name: SEX
variables.label: Sex
variables.description: Sex of the subject

variables.name: RACE
variables.label: Race
variables.description: Race of the subject (from a multi-race code list)

variables.name: ETHNIC
variables.label: Ethnicity
variables.description: The ethnicity of the subject

variables.name: ARMCD
variables.label: Planned Arm Code
variables.description: Short description of the planned arm for a subject

variables.name: ARM
variables.label: Description of Planned Arm
variables.description: Name of the arm to which the subject was assigned

variables.name: ACTARMCD
variables.label: Actual Arm Code
variables.description: Code for the actual arm for a subject

variables.name: ACTARM
variables.label: Description of Actual Arm
variables.description: Description of the actual arm for a subject

variables.name: COUNTRY
variables.label: Country
variables.description: Country of the investigational site (ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3)

variables.name: DMDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Collection
variables.description: Date/time of demographic data collection

variables.name: DMDY
variables.label: Study Day of Collection
variables.description: Study day of demographic data collection

domains.name: Adverse Events
domains.description: One record per adverse event per subject. Contains adverse event data.
domains.structure: One record per adverse event per subject

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

variables.name: AESEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number given to ensure uniqueness of subject records

variables.name: AETERM
variables.label: Reported Term for the Adverse Event
variables.description: Verbatim name of the adverse event as reported by the investigator

variables.name: AEMODIFY
variables.label: Modified Reported Term
variables.description: Modified reported term if different from AETERM

variables.name: AEDECOD
variables.label: Dictionary-Derived Term
variables.description: Dictionary-derived term (MedDRA Preferred Term)

variables.name: AEBODSYS
variables.label: Body System or Organ Class
variables.description: Dictionary-derived (MedDRA System Organ Class)

variables.name: AESEV
variables.label: Severity/Intensity
variables.description: The severity or intensity of the event

variables.name: AESER
variables.label: Serious Event
variables.description: Is the event classified as serious?

variables.name: AEACN
variables.label: Action Taken with Study Treatment
variables.description: Action taken with study treatment as a result of the event

variables.name: AEREL
variables.label: Causality
variables.description: Investigator's opinion of the relationship of the event to treatment

variables.name: AEOUT
variables.label: Outcome of Adverse Event
variables.description: Description of the outcome of the event

variables.name: AESCAN
variables.label: Involves Cancer
variables.description: Is the event a cancer?

variables.name: AESCONG
variables.label: Congenital Anomaly or Birth Defect
variables.description: Is the event a congenital anomaly?

variables.name: AESDISAB
variables.label: Persist or Signif Disability/Incapacity
variables.description: Event results in persistent or significant disability?

variables.name: AESDTH
variables.label: Results in Death
variables.description: Is the event associated with the outcome of death?

variables.name: AESHOSP
variables.label: Requires or Prolongs Hospitalization
variables.description: Event requires or prolongs hospitalization?

variables.name: AESLIFE
variables.label: Is Life Threatening
variables.description: Is the event life threatening?

variables.name: AESOD
variables.label: Occurred with Overdose
variables.description: Event occurred with an overdose?

variables.name: AESTDTC
variables.label: Start Date/Time of Adverse Event
variables.description: Start date/time of the adverse event in ISO 8601

variables.name: AEENDTC
variables.label: End Date/Time of Adverse Event
variables.description: End date/time of the adverse event in ISO 8601

variables.name: AESTDY
variables.label: Study Day of Start of Adverse Event
variables.description: Study day of start of adverse event relative to reference date

variables.name: AEENDY
variables.label: Study Day of End of Adverse Event
variables.description: Study day of end of adverse event relative to reference date

variables.name: AEENRF
variables.label: End Relative to Reference Period
variables.description: Describes the end of the event relative to the reference period

domains.name: Concomitant Medications
domains.description: One record per medication per subject. Contains concomitant and prior medication data.
domains.structure: One record per recorded medication occurrence per subject

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

variables.name: CMSEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness of records

variables.name: CMTRT
variables.label: Reported Name of Drug, Med, or Therapy
variables.description: Verbatim medication name as reported

variables.name: CMMODIFY
variables.label: Modified Reported Name
variables.description: Modified reported name, if different from CMTRT

variables.name: CMDECOD
variables.label: Standardized Medication Name
variables.description: Standardized or dictionary-derived name (WHODrug preferred name)

variables.name: CMCAT
variables.label: Category for Medication
variables.description: Used to define a category of medication

variables.name: CMSCAT
variables.label: Subcategory for Medication
variables.description: Used to define a subcategory of medication

variables.name: CMPRESP
variables.label: CM Pre-Specified
variables.description: Was medication pre-specified on CRF?

variables.name: CMOCCUR
variables.label: CM Occurrence
variables.description: Did the medication occur? Used with CMPRESP

variables.name: CMDOSE
variables.label: Dose per Administration
variables.description: Amount of medication administered per dose

variables.name: CMDOSU
variables.label: Dose Units
variables.description: Units for CMDOSE

variables.name: CMDOSFRQ
variables.label: Dosing Frequency per Interval
variables.description: Usually expressed as the number of doses per time period

variables.name: CMROUTE
variables.label: Route of Administration
variables.description: Route of administration

variables.name: CMSTDTC
variables.label: Start Date/Time of Medication
variables.description: Start date/time in ISO 8601

variables.name: CMENDTC
variables.label: End Date/Time of Medication
variables.description: End date/time in ISO 8601

variables.name: CMSTDY
variables.label: Study Day of Start of Medication
variables.description: Study day of start of medication

variables.name: CMENDY
variables.label: Study Day of End of Medication
variables.description: Study day of end of medication

variables.name: CMINDC
variables.label: Indication
variables.description: Indication for the medication, can be linked to AE

domains.name: Exposure
domains.description: One record per constant-dosing interval per subject. Contains study drug exposure data.
domains.structure: One record per constant-dosing interval per subject

domains.name: Laboratory Test Results
domains.description: One record per lab test per time point per subject. Contains laboratory test result data.
domains.structure: One record per lab test per time point per subject

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier
variables.description: Identifier used to uniquely identify a subject across all studies

variables.name: LBSEQ
variables.label: Sequence Number
variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness

variables.name: LBTESTCD
variables.label: Lab Test or Examination Short Name
variables.description: Short name of the lab test (e.g., ALT, AST, CREAT)

variables.name: LBTEST
variables.label: Lab Test or Examination Name
variables.description: Verbatim name of the test (e.g., Alanine Aminotransferase)

variables.name: LBCAT
variables.label: Category for Lab Test
variables.description: Used to define a category of lab tests (e.g., HEMATOLOGY, CHEMISTRY)

variables.name: LBSCAT
variables.label: Subcategory for Lab Test
variables.description: Subcategory for the lab test

variables.name: LBORRES
variables.label: Result or Finding in Original Units

variables.name: LBORRESU
variables.label: Original Units
variables.description: Original units for LBORRES

variables.name: LBORNRLO
variables.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Orig Unit
variables.description: Lower limit of the reference range in original units

variables.name: LBORNRHI
variables.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Orig Unit
variables.description: Upper limit of the reference range in original units

variables.name: LBSTRESC
variables.label: Character Result/Finding in Std Format
variables.description: Contains the result value for all findings in character format

variables.name: LBSTRESN
variables.label: Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units
variables.description: Numeric result value in standard units

variables.name: LBSTRESU
variables.label: Standard Units
variables.description: Standardized units for LBSTRESN

variables.name: LBSTNRLO
variables.label: Reference Range Lower Limit-Std Units
variables.description: Lower limit of the reference range in standard units

variables.name: LBSTNRHI
variables.label: Reference Range Upper Limit-Std Units
variables.description: Upper limit of the reference range in standard units

variables.name: LBNRIND
variables.label: Reference Range Indicator
variables.description: Indicates where the value falls relative to the reference range (NORMAL, LOW, HIGH)

variables.name: LBSPEC
variables.label: Specimen Type
variables.description: Type of specimen (e.g., BLOOD, SERUM, URINE, PLASMA)

variables.name: LBMETHOD
variables.label: Method of Test or Examination
variables.description: Method of the laboratory test

variables.name: LBBLFL
variables.label: Baseline Flag
variables.description: Indicator for whether the result is a baseline value

variables.name: LBFAST
variables.label: Fasting Status
variables.description: Was the subject fasting for this specimen collection?

variables.name: VISITNUM
variables.label: Visit Number
variables.description: Numeric version of VISIT

variables.name: VISIT
variables.label: Visit Name
variables.description: Protocol-defined description of clinical encounter

variables.name: LBDTC
variables.label: Date/Time of Specimen Collection
variables.description: Date/time of specimen collection in ISO 8601

variables.name: LBDY
variables.label: Study Day of Specimen Collection
variables.description: Study day of specimen collection

domains.description: One record per vital sign measurement per time point per subject.
domains.structure: One record per vital sign per time point per subject

variables.name: STUDYID
variables.label: Study Identifier
variables.description: Unique identifier for a study

variables.name: DOMAIN
variables.label: Domain Abbreviation
variables.description: Two-character abbreviation for the domain

variables.name: USUBJID
variables.label: Unique Subject Identifier

variables.name: VSSEQ

variables.description: Sequence number to ensure uniqueness

variables.name: VSTEST

variables.description: Verbatim name of the vital signs test

variables.name: VSCAT
variables.label: Category of Vital Signs

variables.name: VSPOS

variables.description: Position of the subject during observations (e.g., STANDING, SITTING, SUPINE)

variables.description: Character result in standard format

variables.name: VSSTRESN

variables.description: Numeric result/finding in standard units

variables.name: VSBLFL

variables.description: About whether this is a baseline value

variables.name: VISITNUM

variables.description: Numeric visit

variables.name: VISIT

variables.description: Protocol-defined name of encounter

variables.name: VSDTC

variables.name: VSDY

codelists.name: Race
values: NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER

cross_domain_relationships.description: Each adverse event in AE links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each medication record in CM links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each exposure record in EX links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each lab result in LB links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Each vital sign measurement in VS links to a subject in DM via USUBJID
Concomitant medications may be taken in response to adverse events (CM.CMINDC references AE)
Adverse events may result in changes to study treatment exposure (AEACN: DOSE REDUCED, DRUG WITHDRAWN, etc.)
Abnormal lab values (LBNRIND = HIGH/LOW) may correlate with or trigger adverse events
Laboratory tests are often scheduled relative to treatment exposure to monitor drug effects
Vital signs are often measured relative to dosing events to monitor treatment impact

standard_flow.description: CDASH (Clinical Data Acquisition Standards Harmonization) defines collection standards that map to SDTM tabulation domains
SDTM tabulation datasets are transformed into ADaM analysis datasets for statistical analysis
ADaM datasets are used to produce Tables, Listings, and Figures for regulatory submissions
